hero.name: MARTÍ
hero.intro: I conceive systems where software and hardware converge with industrial precision. Specialized in the architecture of efficient and resilient solutions.

degree.title: Telecommunications & Electronic Engineering
degree.description: Specializing in electronic systems and software integration.

worldline.title: Full-Stack Intern
worldline.description: Developing and maintaining payment processing systems.

projects.title: PROJECTS
projects.description: A sample of systems developed with technical rigor, from RF hardware to AI accelerators.

cpu-practice.title: FPGA miniCPU Design & Implementation
cpu-practice.description: Design of essential CPU parts using the DE10-Lite kit. Introduction to VHDL and FPGA programming.

oop-practice.title: Object-Oriented Design & Programming
oop-practice.description: Introduction to Java by programming an application with a simple front-end and back-end stack to run a minigame.
oop-practice.longDescription: SOLID principles, software modeling and analysis, OOP programming principles, data persistence, concurrency.

chat-pdf.title: ChatPDF
chat-pdf.description: Small program using Python and Generative AI APIs to create an academic-level ChatPDF.
chat-pdf.longDescription: Through the 'AI Applied to Robotics' course, covered various ML, DL concepts and the use of third-party APIs to analyze data and obtain results from academic databases.

ls-battleship.title: Assembly: PIC18 Digital System (Battleship)
ls-battleship.description: Manual design and implementation with 74-family chips on a prototype board and expansion with a PIC18F using assembly.
ls-battleship.longDescription: During this practice, we covered digital electronics and gate logic concepts, assembly-level programming, digital systems design, and bug resolution in measurement labs to implement a minigame: the classic battleship. To achieve this, part of the board had to be implemented with very basic 74-family components such as counters, registers, synchronisms in soft soldering. Once the first input part was achieved: matrix keypad, joystick; an SSS (state machine) is in charge of controlling the game logic and system outputs: speaker, matrix display, pwm speaker, UART; all implemented in Assembly.

ls-smartlight.title: C: PIC18 Digital Cooperative System (SmartLight)
ls-smartlight.description: Design and implementation on a PIC18 of cooperative peripheral control, integrated into PCB.
ls-smartlight.longDescription: In this project, we implemented a system that, through an RFID reader, can control the light level of a room. Programmed in C and cooperatively, the goal of this project has been to learn to manage time at the microprocessor scale and the memory of a chip as small as a PIC18.

the-citadel.title: Operating Systems: The Citadel
the-citadel.description: Design and implementation of a multitasking distributed system based on a network architecture for alliance and trade management.
the-citadel.longDescription: Development of a complex ecosystem consisting of Maester processes (servers) and Envoy processes (agents) that communicate through sockets and custom protocols. The project addresses concurrency management, process synchronization, shared memory use, management of configuration and inventory files, and implementation of robust network protocols with integrity verification (MD5SUM).

gnss-frontend.title: Active GNSS Front-End Design
gnss-frontend.description: Design of an active reception system for GNSS signals (In progress).
